e102.name: Tartrazine
e102.note: Azo dye linked to hyperactivity in children (Southampton study); EU requires a warning label.

e104.name: Quinoline Yellow
e104.note: Azo-type colour; EU warning label for effects on children's attention.

e110.name: Sunset Yellow FCF
e110.note: Azo dye; EU warning label for possible effects on children's activity and attention.

e120.name: Cochineal / Carmine
e120.note: Natural colour; can trigger allergic reactions in sensitive people.

e122.name: Azorubine (Carmoisine)
e122.note: Azo dye; EU warning label for possible effects on children.

e123.name: Amaranth
e123.note: Azo dye banned in the United States; restricted in the EU.

e124.name: Ponceau 4R
e124.note: Azo dye; EU warning label for possible effects on children.

e129.name: Allura Red AC
e129.note: Azo dye; EU warning label for possible effects on children.

e150d.name: Sulphite Ammonia Caramel
e150d.note: May contain 4-methylimidazole; intake limits recommended by EFSA.

e171.name: Titanium Dioxide
e171.note: Banned as a food additive in the EU (2022) after EFSA could not rule out genotoxicity.

e211.name: Sodium Benzoate
e211.note: Can form benzene when combined with ascorbic acid (vitamin C).

e220.name: Sulphur Dioxide
e220.note: Common allergen; can trigger reactions in asthmatics.

e223.name: Sodium Metabisulphite
e223.note: Sulphite; may cause reactions in sensitive individuals.

e250.name: Sodium Nitrite
e250.note: Can form nitrosamines; processed meats classified carcinogenic to humans by IARC.

e249.name: Potassium Nitrite
e249.note: Can form nitrosamines; linked to processed-meat cancer risk (IARC).

e251.name: Sodium Nitrate
e251.note: Converts to nitrite; nitrosamine formation concern.

e252.name: Potassium Nitrate
e252.note: Converts to nitrite; nitrosamine formation concern.

e320.name: Butylated Hydroxyanisole (BHA)
e320.note: Classified as possibly carcinogenic to humans (IARC Group 2B).

e321.name: Butylated Hydroxytoluene (BHT)
e321.note: Antioxidant; Sage rates it high among synthetic phenolic antioxidants.

e338.name: Phosphoric Acid
e338.note: High phosphate intake associated with bone and kidney concerns.

e339.name: Sodium Phosphates
e339.note: Added phosphates; EFSA set a group intake limit.

e407.name: Carrageenan
e407.note: Some studies suggest digestive inflammation; under ongoing review.

e433.name: Polysorbate 80
e433.note: Emulsifier; animal studies suggest possible gut-microbiome effects.

e450.name: Diphosphates
e450.note: Added phosphates; EFSA set a group intake limit.

e466.name: Carboxymethyl Cellulose
e466.note: Emulsifier; emerging research on gut-microbiome effects.

e471.name: Mono- and Diglycerides of Fatty Acids

e621.name: Monosodium Glutamate (MSG)
e621.note: Flavour enhancer; some report sensitivity, though evidence is limited.

e950.name: Acesulfame K
e950.note: Artificial sweetener; long-term studies remain limited.

e951.name: Aspartame
e951.note: IARC Group 2B (2023); JECFA kept the ADI. Mixed but closely watched.

e952.name: Cyclamate
e952.note: Banned in the United States; permitted with limits in the EU.

e955.name: Sucralose
e955.note: Artificial sweetener; some evidence of effects when heated.

e968.name: Erythritol
e968.note: Recent studies suggest a possible association with cardiovascular events.
